I’m a Senior AI / Cloud Engineer with 7+ years of experience building scalable cloud platforms, AI systems, and high-performance data pipelines across AWS and Kubernetes environments. My work focuses on designing and deploying production-grade AI systems, including LLM-powered applications, intelligent analytics platforms, and distributed cloud architectures that process millions of records daily. Currently, I build enterprise AI and data platforms, developing high-throughput APIs, Snowflake data pipelines, and LLM-driven analytics systems that help teams make faster, data-driven decisions. # ESG Regulatory Compliance & Data Governance Analyst
...
Employer Intercontinental Exchange Holdings, Inc. Location Dublin, Ireland Salary EUR 50,000.00 - 60,000.00 per year Closing date 29 Jul 2026
...
Sector Engineering, IT, Security Job Role Corporate Governance Job Type Permanent

## Job Details
...
Job DescriptionJob PurposeICE Climate is establishing a dedicated Data Integrity & Governance (DIG) team in Dublin to ensure compliance with the European Securities and Markets Authority (ESMA) Regulation on ESG Rating Providers (Regulation (EU) 2024/3005). As an Analyst on the DIG team, you will work at the intersection of Data, Product, and Compliance to support the governance, validation, and oversight of Sustainable Finance data workflows and climate risk scoring methodologies.This hybrid role combines regulatory rigor with product innovation, ensuring our solutions are both compliant and commercially compelling. You will engage directly with clients and prospects to gather feedback, understand use cases, and translate market needs into product enhancements that meet ESMA expectations.Responsibilities

As a Data Scientist in Global Quant Research at Intercontinental Exchange (ICE), I partner with researchers and product teams to deliver clean, and audit-ready model inputs—blending statistical modeling with production-grade data workflows. I also work on data engineering initiatives to automate workflows, freeing up time and resources for deeper exploration of modeling, analytics, and algorithm development. Before ICE, I worked in buy-side environments on factor modeling and systematic research, translating quantitative ideas into robust, testable workflows and bridging financial theory with practical implementation. Outside of work, I enjoy crypto trading—combining on-chain analysis with macroeconomic trends and changes in the regulatory landscape and exploring data-driven approaches to digital asset research, including the design and testing of time-series strategies.

results.title: Jiateng Wang
highlights:
Senior Quantitative Engineer at ICE | Expert in Futures/Options Pricing Models | Georgia Tech Alumnus | Skilled in Math & Programming
...
Senior Quantitative Engineer at [ICE](https://www.linkedin.com/company/intercontinentalexchange-inc-)
...
As a Senior Quantitative Engineer at Intercontinental Exchange (ICE) since February 2022, I specialize in developing and testing sophisticated financial models. My role involves creating reference implementations for testing platform applications and reviewing technical requirements to align with quantitative model terminology. I am proficient in designing comprehensive test strategies, scenarios, and cases, and adept at implementing, maintaining, and troubleshooting test harnesses for various quantitative models, including risk factor construction, moment matching, and volatility surface modeling using LSSVI/RSVI techniques. I have a strong focus on futures and options pricing models and the integration of advanced mathematical concepts into practical financial applications. My expertise extends to creating detailed test plans that define scope, resources, dependencies, risks, and strategies for testing complex systems. I ensure the integrity of applications through rigorous functional, regression, system, and integration testing. I hold dual master's degrees in Computational Science and Engineering (CSE) and Quantitative and Computational Finance (QCF) from Georgia Institute of Technology. My technical skills are complemented by a passion for sports such as climbing, skiing, and golfing.

results.title: Andrei Lupica
highlights:
AI Engineer | LLM Systems, RAG & MLOps | Full-Stack AI Engineer @ London Stock Exchange Group | Data Scientist @ Deutsche Telekom | BCI research cited in Elsevier (2026)
...
I am a Full-Stack AI Engineer and Data Scientist specializing in LLMs, RAG, and production-ready NLP systems. Recently, my team and I secured ist Place at the Ultrahack DEFINE 2025 Hackathon (powered by Sako) and I published a Brain-Computer Interface (BCI) thesis that was CITED in a 2026 Elsevier journal review paper. Currently, at the London Stock Exchange and Deutsche Telekom, I design and deploy scalable AI architectures. My work focuses on retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), function calling, evaluation pipelines, and explainable NLP to make large language models more accurate and efficient in real-world applications. Over the past two years, I’ve bridged the gap between academic research (B.Eng. in AI & Data Engineering from SAMK, Feb 2026) and industry execution. I have delivered FastAPI-based AI services at Mindrift and automated robotics workflows at RoboAI using ROS and multimodal sensor fusion. What drives me is building AI that moves beyond prototypes to genuinely help people work smarter. I love collaborating across disciplines, simplifying complex architectures, and finding elegant solutions to hard problems. My Technical Toolkit: Core AI/ML: Python, PyTorch, TensorFlow, LangChain, Scikit-Learn Architecture & MLOps: AWS, Databricks, FastAPI, Evaluation Pipelines, Dagster, Cohere, ElasticSearch Domain Expertise: LLMs, NLP, RAG, Computer Vision, Signal Processing (EEG/EMG), Robotics (ROS) Always curious and results-driven, I’m looking to connect with innovative AI teams where engineering meets creativity and research meets real-world execution.

Atlanta, GA, USA

With the support and investment needed to explore new frontiers in generative AI, you'll be working alongside talented colleagues, innovating and leading projects that will transform how we manage risk and serve our customers.
...

We are seeking a highly technical individual contributor with extensive hands-on experience, as we are an applied data science team operating in an agile and production-oriented environment. # Machine Learning / AI Engineer
...
Job Summary Our client is seeking a Machine Learning / AI Engineer to design, build, and operate production-grade ML systems that drive real business impact with our client. In this role, you will own the full model lifecycle—from problem framing and data engineering through training, deployment, and ongoing monitoring—while collaborating closely with product, data engineering, and software teams. You will be expected to communicate complex trade-offs clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders and champion responsible AI practices across the organization. Core Responsibilities
...
- Partner with stakeholders to frame business problems as ML/AI use cases, define success metrics, and identify required data
...
- Build and iterate on models using classification, regression, ranking, clustering, anomaly detection, and NLP approaches
...
- Perform feature engineering, dataset creation, labeling strategies, and model evaluation with scientific rigor
...
- Implement model interpretability, bias assessment, and responsible AI techniques where applicable
...
- Build production-grade ML services and pipelines (batch + real-time), ensuring performance, reliability, and maintainability
...
- Deploy models to cloud environments using CI/CD and infrastructure-as-code best practices
...
- Implement monitoring for data drift, model drift, latency, throughput, cost, and model quality
...
- Maintain versioning for datasets, features, models, and experiments to ensure repeatability and governance
...
- Collaborate with data engineering to create robust data pipelines and ensure data quality
...
- Integrate ML models into applications through APIs, event streams, or workflow orchestration
...
- Document architecture, operational runbooks, and model cards; participate in design reviews and knowledge sharing
...
Required Skills/Experience (Must-Haves)
...
- 4+ years building and integrating APIs (FastAPI / Flask) and 3rd-party API integrations
- 3+ years (Mid) / 5+ years (Senior) shipping ML systems into production—not just notebooks
...
- Strong Python skills and hands-on experience with ML libraries: scikit-learn, PyTorch, TensorFlow, XGBoost
...
- Data processing and analytics with Pandas, NumPy, SQL, and Spark
...
- Solid ML fundamentals: bias/variance trade-offs, evaluation metrics, cross-validation, feature engineering, error analysis
...
- Containerization with Docker and CI/CD pipeline management
...
- Version control with Git / GitHub / GitLab
...
- Strong SQL skills (SQL Server or Oracle)
...
- Scripting in Python, Bash, and/or PowerShell
...
- Ability to communicate trade-offs and results clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders
...
- Experience documenting architecture, model cards, and operational runbooks
...
Preferred Skills/Experience (Nice-to-Haves)
...
- Experience with workflow automation tools and orchestration platforms
- Background in highly regulated or financial services environments
- Familiarity with cloud ML platforms (AWS SageMaker, Azure ML, GCP Vertex AI)
- Knowledge of Disaster Recovery / Business Continuity Planning processes
...

results.title: Data Scientist (AI Data & LLM Specialist) | Wfh
highlights:
Data Scientist (AI Data & LLM Specialist) | Wfh
...
# Data Scientist (AI Data & LLM Specialist)
...
Join the core team at Eclipse, where we’re building an AI agent-first marketplace that connects intelligence with real-world tasks, starting with data collection and labeling. We are seeking a Data Scientist to establish the foundation for how our data is labeled, processed, and prepared for consumption by next-generation Large Language Models (LLMs). Your work will be critical in transforming our raw data collections into valuable, AI-ready datasets.
...
- Proven experience as a Data Scientist or Machine Learning Engineer with a focus on data quality and preparation.
- Strong understanding of data labeling methodologies and hands-on experience with data annotation platforms and workflows.
...
- Demonstrated experience preparing datasets for training and fine-tuning Large Language Models (LLMs), including knowledge of techniques like tokenization, embeddings, and NER.
...
- Proficiency in Python and common data science libraries (e.g., Pandas, NumPy, Scikit-learn, spaCy, Hugging Face).
...
- Experience using APIs/SDKs to automate data annotation and active learning loops.
...
- Responsibilities
...
- Develop Data Labeling Strategies: Design and document a formal data annotation strategy, including clear, scalable, and efficient guidelines for labeling our data. Define and enforce quality metrics, including inter-annotator agreement.
...
- Optimize for LLM Consumption: Research, define, and prototype the optimal data formats, structures, and pre-processing steps required for fine-tuning and training LLMs on our datasets.
...
- Data Quality Analysis: Establish automated processes and metrics to analyze the quality of both raw and labeled data, providing feedback to improve our data collection and labeling workflows.
...
- Collaborate with Engineering: Work closely with the engineering team to guide the implementation of data processing pipelines and ensure the data infrastructure meets the needs of ML applications.
...
- Nice-to-Haves
- Experience with audio data processing and relevant libraries.
- Familiarity with data annotation platforms and tools.
- Knowledge of modern MLOps principles and practices.
- Experience with large language model data curation and Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) pipelines.
